title: History Editing, Rebase, and Selective Transfer · Conflict Triage
summary: Prepare a reviewable local series while preserving authorship intent, conflict evidence, and a recovery reference. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a history-rewrite plan and verification graph and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.
Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, drop, exec, and reorder with dependency and test awareness.
Limit history rewriting using collaboration state, force-with-lease, recovery refs, and explicit coordination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.

Inspect the Rebase Replay trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities.

content.text: For Rebase Replay, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Rebase Replay precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Rebase moves existing commit objects without changing their IDs.

stimulus.title: Rebase Replay case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.text: Rebase moves existing commit objects without changing their IDs.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain rebase as selecting commits, resetting a base, and replaying new commits with new identities. Artifact under review: a history-rewrite plan and verification graph.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Rebase Replay, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-rewrite-verification-94mxnz" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rewrite Verification into observable performance evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Rewrite Verification: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Local series has commits parse → test → docs atop old main; new main changes the parser contract. Plan replay, conflict tests, range-diff, recovery ref, and force-with-lease boundary.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare old and rewritten ranges by patch, tree, tests, metadata, and range-diff before replacing a shared reference.
